user_prompt: Add Google OAuth login to simplarr's web companion. Today the configurator is CLI-only, but I want a tiny web view that lets me see the configured stack status and re-run the configurator from a button. For auth, just Google OAuth — no signup form, no other providers. The login is gating access; once logged in, the user is implicitly authorized. No multi-user roles. Scope is login + a single status page; no settings UI yet.

GitWeave's stage runner has grown to 1,200 lines mixing scheduling, retry policy, and result persistence. Split it along those three concerns into separate modules with explicit interfaces between them. No behavior change — purely a structural refactor with the existing tests as the safety net. Keep the public API of the stage runner unchanged; callers should not need updates.

trafficGame has no CI yet. Set up GitHub Actions to run on every PR: install deps, run the typecheck, run the test suite, and post the result as a check. No deployment, no caching strategy beyond the default action cache, no matrix beyond a single Node 22 runner. The point is the basic guardrail; we'll layer in caching and matrix builds later if the runtime hurts.

Add a `simplarr migrate` subcommand to the existing CLI. It runs schema migrations against the local indexer config — taking a from-version and to-version, applying the diffs in order, and reporting what changed. Must work in both the bash and powershell configurators (parity is non-negotiable for simplarr). Idempotent re-runs should be a no-op. Rollback is out of scope for v1 — log loudly and exit non-zero on failure.

healarr's request list endpoint takes 4-5 seconds when there are 200+ pending requests. Profile to confirm the cause is unindexed sorting + N+1 fetches, then fix both: add a covering index on (status, created_at), and replace the N+1 with a single join. Target is sub-500ms p95 for the same data shape. Backfill / migration risk: the index is additive and online, so deployment is safe.

env-optimiser has one module (the workspace state model) using raw SQL via `pg`. Port it to Drizzle to match the rest of the codebase. Keep the same schema — this is a code-side migration only, no DB changes. Provide a rollback path: keep the raw-SQL implementation behind a feature flag for one release so we can flip back if Drizzle has performance surprises. Acceptance: existing integration tests pass against both implementations during the deprecation window.

trafficGame has zero tests. Set up vitest, write smoke tests covering: game initialization, one round of basic gameplay (move + score), and game-over detection. Aim for ~60% coverage on the core game-loop module — not the rendering layer yet. Wire the tests into the CI pipeline (assume CI is already set up). I want a baseline; we'll layer in more coverage as we touch each module.

GitWeave has no public docs site. Set up a docusaurus site under `docs/` that pulls from the existing markdown files in the repo (architecture.md, contributing.md, the ADRs). Auto-deploy to GitHub Pages on merge to main. No custom theming yet — the default is fine. Goal is a browsable, search-indexable view of the existing docs without copying content.

terraform-provider-betterado is a Go fork of microsoft/terraform-provider-azuredevops that targets the ADO 7.1 createable surface. The substrate I want queued THIS session is narrow: only INIT-01 (release_definition test substrate + deployment_gates). `azuredevops/internal/service/release/resource_release_definition.go` EXISTS but has zero unit tests; add a `release_sdk_mock`/gomock substrate, then add `pre_deployment_gates` and `post_deployment_gates` schema blocks with expand/flatten + tests + docs. Quality gate: `go test ./azuredevops/internal/service/release/...` passes + `go build -mod=vendor ./...` exits 0. Do NOT queue the other 18 betterado initiatives — they are out of scope for this brief.

Queue the entire ADO 7.1 createable surface for terraform-provider-betterado as a 20-initiative program: substrate (release_definition, task_group, test plan core) plus 17 dependent initiatives (servicehook subscriptions, notification subscriptions, audit streams, pipelines API, git PR, dashboard widgets, secure files, env/VM resources, build retention, feed views, workitem collaboration, serviceendpoint longtail, PAT token mgmt, etc). Each initiative ships its own gomock test substrate + docs + example. Share project-level constraints (gate command, fixture rules, scope-guard) across initiatives — don't copy-paste them. Aggregate-cost footprint is informational; the operator decides whether to split the queue after seeing PLAN.md.
